title: Russian Shadow Fleet Tanker Grounds Near Denmark After Sanctions Evasion
summary: Russian tanker Eagle S grounds in Baltic Sea near Denmark. Incident follows sanctions targeting shadow fleet. Danish authorities detain vessel amid environmental concerns.
contents:
## Russian Shadow Fleet Tanker Grounds Near Denmark After Sanctions Evasion

### Overview
Russian tanker Eagle S grounds in Baltic Sea near Denmark. Incident follows sanctions targeting shadow fleet. Danish authorities detain vessel amid environmental concerns.

### Event Details
Danish authorities report tanker Eagle S, part of Russia's shadow fleet, runs aground near Stevns peninsula in Baltic Sea. Vessel carries 30,000 tons of fuel oil, raises spill risks. Coast guard deploys vessels, conducts oil slick surveillance, confirms no immediate leakage. Eagle S operates under sanctions evasion tactics, frequently changes flags and ownership. Incident occurs 23 April 2026, prompts swift response from Danish rescue coordination center. Crew of 24 remains safe, authorities evacuate non-essential personnel. Environmental agency monitors water quality, prepares containment booms. Russia shadow fleet continues transporting sanctioned oil despite Western bans. Eagle S previously sails under Panama flag, switches to unknown registry days before grounding. Navigational error, possibly strong currents, contributes to mishap. Local fishermen report vessel drifts close to shore before anchoring. Danish navy supports operations, investigates potential sabotage links. Incident disrupts regional shipping lanes, forces rerouting of ferries. Shadow fleet employs dark fleet tactics, turns off AIS transponders to evade detection. International maritime organizations condemn operations, urge stricter enforcement. Eagle S refloats efforts commence with tugboats pulling vessel off sandbar. Salvage teams pump fuel to reduce weight, prevent capsizing. Incident highlights vulnerabilities in Baltic Sea routes amid geopolitical tensions. Authorities seize documents, probe compliance with EU sanctions. Similar groundings plague shadow fleet, underscores enforcement challenges.

### Source Context
@ShipNews (ShipNews) covers this category of developments. The account focuses on shipping vessel incident grounding sanctions detention port disruption.
